trickyParts: Pyright TypedDict narrowing: block assignments in _apply_replacements_to_request_messages needed # type: ignore[typeddict-unknown-key] because Pyright can't narrow AnthropicContentBlock union via dict.get() checks. Used same pattern as existing on_anthropic_response code.
Replacement counting: counting occurrences before replacement (not after) to get accurate total_replacements count, since the replaced text may not contain the original pattern.

context.record_event vs add_span_event: initial implementation used add_span_event (OTEL-only) for request side; corrected to use context.record_event for both request and response so interventions appear in the dashboard activity monitor.
deviations: Renamed response event from 'policy.anthropic_string_replacement.content_transformed' to 'policy.string_replacement.response_modified' for naming consistency with new request event. This is a minor breaking change for anyone monitoring that specific event name.

PR created to trajectory-labs-pbc/luthien-proxy (fork) rather than LuthienResearch/luthien-proxy (upstream) due to GitHub integration permissions. PR #3 at https://github.com/trajectory-labs-pbc/luthien-proxy/pull/3

apply_to uses 'request'/'response'/'both' enum (default 'response') rather than apply_to_requests/apply_to_responses booleans (default both True) as suggested in dispatch prompt. The enum approach follows the issue spec and is more explicit about intent.
openQuestions: The response event rename ('policy.anthropic_string_replacement.content_transformed' -> 'policy.string_replacement.response_modified') may need a migration note if anyone is monitoring that event name in dashboards.

Streaming path (on_anthropic_stream_event) does not emit record_event per chunk — only the non-streaming on_anthropic_response path does. This is consistent with existing behavior but worth noting for completeness.
